obligation_or_commitment_summary: Defines 'pandemic emergency' as a PHEIC caused by a communicable disease with wide geographical spread, health system overload, substantial social/economic disruption, and need for enhanced international action — a legally operative definition that activates specific Agreement obligations
legal_status: adopted agreement provision
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; National IHR Authority / National IHR Focal Point; Legislature / parliament
notes: Adopted text; definition is operative and may require domestic alignment with IHR 2005 pandemic emergency determination per Art. 12 IHR; update if IHR term is further amended

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Defines 'pandemic-related health products' as those relevant health products needed for prevention, preparedness and response to pandemic emergencies — cross-cutting definition applicable to supply chain, procurement, R&D, PABS, and access provisions
legal_status: adopted agreement provision
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; Regulatory authority for medicines and health products; National public health authority
notes: Adopted text; definition is operationally dependent on 'relevant health products' as defined in IHR (2005) Art. 1 (2024 amendment); PABS Annex will further specify scope for pathogens

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Defines 'universal health coverage' as access to the full range of quality health services without financial hardship, covering the full continuum of essential health services — anchors the equity framing of the Agreement
legal_status: adopted agreement provision
possible_domestic_actor: TBD after country mapping
notes: Adopted text; definitional only; no standalone domestic obligation

obligation_or_commitment_summary: The objective of the Agreement — guided by equity — is to prevent, prepare for and respond to pandemics; provisions apply both during and between pandemics unless otherwise specified
legal_status: framework obligation
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; National IHR Authority / National IHR Focal Point
notes: Adopted text; objective clause; does not stand alone as a domestic obligation but defines the interpretive frame for all substantive provisions

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Parties shall be guided by: sovereign right to legislate; full respect for dignity, human rights and fundamental freedoms; international humanitarian law; equity as goal, principle and outcome; solidarity; and best available science and evidence
legal_status: adopted agreement provision
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; Legislature / parliament; Ministry of Foreign Affairs
notes: Adopted text; principles clause; has interpretive legal force; equity and human rights principles may require domestic legal review for compliance in implementation

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Parties shall take steps through international collaboration to progressively strengthen pandemic prevention and surveillance measures and capacities, consistent with IHR (2005)
legal_status: cooperation commitment
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; Ministry of Foreign Affairs; National IHR Authority / National IHR Focal Point
notes: Adopted text; requires ratification/implementation review; IHR consistency clause creates cross-instrument obligations

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall develop or strengthen comprehensive multisectoral national pandemic prevention and surveillance plans consistent with IHR (2005), covering areas in Art. 4.2(a)–(j), taking into account public health priorities and relevant international standards
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; National public health authority; National IHR Authority / National IHR Focal Point; Subnational governments
notes: Adopted text; requires ratification/implementation review; multisectoral plan must address at minimum items (a)–(j); subject to national circumstances and resource availability

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall take measures to prevent emerging and re-emerging infectious diseases, promoting collaboration across sectors to identify and address drivers of infectious disease at the human-animal-environment interface
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; Agriculture / animal health authority; Environmental authority; One Health coordination mechanism
notes: Adopted text; cross-sectoral mandate requires institutional coordination mechanisms; possible overlap with One Health obligations in Art. 5

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall take measures to prevent infectious disease transmission between animals and humans, including measures to identify and reduce pandemic risks associated with human-animal interactions and prevention at source
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Agriculture / animal health authority; Ministry of Health; Environmental authority; One Health coordination mechanism
notes: Adopted text; requires coordination with animal health sector; domestic legislation may need amendment if One Health coordination mechanisms are absent

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall establish coordinated multisectoral surveillance to detect and assess pathogens with pandemic potential — including zoonotic and AMR pathogens — and share outputs across sectors within its territory
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: National public health authority; National laboratory network; Agriculture / animal health authority; Environmental authority
notes: Adopted text; requires ratification/implementation review; intersectoral information sharing may require legal frameworks; closely related to IHR core capacity CC1 and CC5

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall strengthen community-level mechanisms to detect and report unusual public health events to relevant authorities within its territory for early containment
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: National public health authority; Subnational governments; National IHR Authority / National IHR Focal Point
notes: Adopted text; requires community-level surveillance architecture; subnational coordination often requires domestic legal framework

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall strengthen efforts to ensure access to safe water, sanitation and hygiene for all, including in hard-to-reach areas, as part of pandemic prevention
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; Subnational governments
notes: Adopted text; WASH obligation is a long-standing SDG commitment; domestic anchoring depends on existing infrastructure legislation

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall strengthen effective routine immunization programmes, including maintaining high coverage, timely supplementary vaccination, public awareness of immunization, and strengthening supply chains and immunization systems
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; National public health authority; Subnational governments
notes: Adopted text; requires ratification/implementation review; immunization programme obligations may require regulatory framework for vaccine approval and mandatory schedules

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall implement infection prevention and control measures, including safe management of medical waste in health care facilities and long-term care facilities
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; National public health authority; Regulatory authority for medicines and health products
notes: Adopted text; IPC measures often require health facility regulation and medical waste management legislation

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall develop, strengthen and maintain capacities for surveillance, risk assessment and prevention of vector-borne diseases that may lead to pandemic emergencies, including consideration of social, demographic and environmental factors
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: National public health authority; National laboratory network; Environmental authority
notes: Adopted text; may require dedicated surveillance programmes and inter-agency coordination

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall implement laboratory biological risk management, including biosafety and biosecurity training and practices, and ensuring safety of transportation in accordance with applicable international and national regulations
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: National laboratory network; National public health authority; Customs / border authorities
notes: Adopted text; requires legal and regulatory framework for biosafety, biosecurity and dangerous goods transport

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall take measures to address public health risks associated with AMR, including facilitating affordable and equitable access to antimicrobials and promoting appropriate, prudent and responsible use across relevant sectors
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; Regulatory authority for medicines and health products; Agriculture / animal health authority
notes: Adopted text; AMR obligations may require national action plans and prescription/dispensing regulatory frameworks

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Parties shall promote a One Health approach for pandemic prevention, preparedness and response that is coherent, integrated, coordinated and collaborative across all relevant organizations, sectors and actors, consistent with national law and applicable international law
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: One Health coordination mechanism; Ministry of Health; Agriculture / animal health authority; Environmental authority
notes: Adopted text; promotes One Health as a cross-sectoral governance requirement; may require establishing legal basis for One Health coordination mechanism

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Parties shall take measures to identify and address drivers of pandemics and emergence of infectious diseases at the human-animal-environment interface, through relevant pandemic prevention, preparedness and response plans
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: One Health coordination mechanism; Agriculture / animal health authority; Environmental authority; Ministry of Health
notes: Adopted text; cross-sectoral measures require institutional basis; domestic legislation for inter-agency coordination may be needed

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall develop, implement and review relevant national policies and strategies that reflect a One Health approach for pandemic prevention, preparedness and response, including community engagement
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: One Health coordination mechanism; Ministry of Health; Agriculture / animal health authority; Environmental authority
notes: Adopted text; requires ratification/implementation review; policy and strategy development may require formal inter-institutional mandate

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Parties shall promote or establish joint training and continuing education programmes for the workforce at the human-animal-environment interface, building relevant and complementary skills in a One Health approach
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; Agriculture / animal health authority; Environmental authority; Science and technology authority
notes: Adopted text; joint training may require inter-ministerial coordination and budgetary planning; can often be achieved administratively

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party, within the means and resources at its disposal, shall develop, strengthen and maintain a resilient health system, particularly primary health care, for pandemic prevention, preparedness and response, taking into account equity and aiming at universal health coverage
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; Subnational governments; Ministry of Finance
notes: Adopted text; health system resilience obligation is broad; may require legislative framework for pandemic-specific health system planning and financing

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall develop or strengthen health system functions for: equitable access to clinical care during pandemics; procurement and supply chain management; laboratory and diagnostic capacities; risk communication and community engagement; and post-pandemic recovery
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; National public health authority; National laboratory network; Public procurement authority
notes: Adopted text; multi-component health system obligation; laboratory and procurement components may require specific regulatory frameworks

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall endeavour to develop, strengthen and maintain national health information systems, in accordance with national law, including use of international data standards for interoperability, based on good data governance
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; National public health authority; Data protection authority
notes: Adopted text; health information systems may require data governance legislation and cross-agency data sharing frameworks

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall monitor its preparedness capacities and periodically assess the functioning, readiness and gaps in its pandemic prevention, preparedness and response capacities
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; National IHR Authority / National IHR Focal Point; National public health authority
notes: Adopted text; periodic assessment is an ongoing monitoring obligation; may be integrated with IHR State Party Self-Assessment reporting

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall take appropriate measures to develop, strengthen, protect, retain and invest in a multidisciplinary, skilled, adequate, trained, domestic health and care workforce, including in humanitarian settings, while maintaining essential health services
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; Subnational governments; Ministry of Finance
notes: Adopted text; health workforce development obligation may require regulatory frameworks for training, licensing and deployment

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall ensure decent work and protect the safety, mental health, well-being and capacity of the health and care workforce, including priority access to pandemic-related health products, non-discrimination, elimination of violence, and work-related injury/disability/death policies
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; Legislature / parliament
notes: Adopted text; decent work and non-discrimination obligations may require labour law and occupational health legislation

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall endeavour to designate or establish national, subnational and/or regional multidisciplinary emergency health teams; Parties shall take measures to strengthen and sustain a skilled global health emergency workforce deployable upon request
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; National public health authority; Subnational governments
notes: Adopted text; designation of emergency health teams may require formal legal establishment and inter-agency mandate

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Parties shall collaborate to minimize the negative impact of health and care workforce migration on health systems, respecting freedom of movement and taking into account the WHO Global Code of Practice on International Recruitment of Health Personnel
legal_status: cooperation commitment
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; Ministry of Foreign Affairs; Legislature / parliament
notes: Adopted text; migration policy dimension may require legal framework provisions on health worker recruitment and bilateral agreements

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Parties shall ensure decent work and a safe environment for essential workers during pandemic emergencies, including coordinated policies for transport and supply chain workers and facilitation of their transit and access to medical care
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; Legislature / parliament; Customs / border authorities
notes: Adopted text; supply chain worker protection requires regulatory framework; cross-border transit facilitation may need diplomatic arrangements

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall strengthen its national and, where appropriate, regional regulatory authority responsible for authorization and approval of pandemic-related health products, including through WHO technical assistance and cooperation with other Parties
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Regulatory authority for medicines and health products; Ministry of Health
notes: Adopted text; regulatory authority strengthening may require legislative establishment and expanded mandate

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall ensure technical capacity and legal, administrative, and financial frameworks for: expedited regulatory review and/or emergency authorization of pandemic-related health products; and effective vigilance to monitor safety and effectiveness
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Regulatory authority for medicines and health products; Ministry of Health; Legislature / parliament
notes: Adopted text; expedited/emergency authorization requires explicit legal basis in domestic medicines law

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall make publicly available and keep updated: information on regulatory processes for pandemic-related health products; and information on products authorized or approved, including relevant additional information
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Regulatory authority for medicines and health products
notes: Adopted text; transparency obligation; can often be achieved through administrative action; legal basis for information disclosure may be needed in some systems

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall endeavour to adopt, where needed, regulatory reliance mechanisms for pandemic-related health products during PHEICs, taking into account relevant guidelines
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Regulatory authority for medicines and health products; Legislature / parliament
notes: Adopted text; regulatory reliance requires legal enabling framework in national medicines law; 'endeavour to adopt' softens the obligation

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall encourage relevant developers and manufacturers to diligently seek regulatory authorizations and approvals from national and/or regional regulatory authorities, including WHO listed authorities, and WHO prequalification
legal_status: implementation-oriented commitment
possible_domestic_actor: Regulatory authority for medicines and health products; Ministry of Health
notes: Adopted text; promotional obligation; primarily directed at encouraging market actors; no direct domestic legislative anchoring required

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Parties shall cooperate to build, strengthen and sustain geographically diverse R&D capacities and institutions, particularly in developing countries, and promote rapid sharing of research information and results during PHEICs
legal_status: cooperation commitment
possible_domestic_actor: Science and technology authority; Ministry of Health; Research institutions
notes: Adopted text; international cooperation commitment; may benefit from domestic framework for international R&D partnerships and data sharing

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Parties shall promote sustained investment in research institutions and networks, scientific programmes and partnerships with developing country researchers, generation of evidence synthesis, sharing of research agendas, capacity-building programmes, and accelerating innovative R&D with biosafety compliance
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Science and technology authority; Ministry of Health; Research institutions; Legislature / parliament
notes: Adopted text; sustained investment implies budgetary planning and possible legislative mandate for R&D funding

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall promote well-designed clinical trials in its jurisdiction during PHEICs, including representative trial populations, sharing of comparator products, and promoting access to products resulting from trials
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Regulatory authority for medicines and health products; Ministry of Health; Science and technology authority
notes: Adopted text; clinical trial facilitation may require legal framework for emergency authorization and expedited ethics review

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall support rapid and transparent publication of clinical trial protocols and other research results related to the implementation of the Agreement
legal_status: cooperation commitment
possible_domestic_actor: Science and technology authority; Research institutions
notes: Adopted text; transparency commitment; may be implemented through funding conditions or regulatory requirements

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall develop and implement national/regional policies regarding provisions in publicly funded R&D grants and contracts for pandemic-related health products that promote timely and equitable access, including licensing/sublicensing, affordable pricing, technology access and adherence to WHO product allocation frameworks
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Science and technology authority; Ministry of Health; Legislature / parliament; Public procurement authority
notes: Adopted text; requires ratification/implementation review; domestic policy on IP conditions in public funding may require legislative or regulatory action

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Parties shall take measures to achieve more equitable geographical distribution and rapid scale-up of global production of pandemic-related health products, including through technology transfer and supply chain measures
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; Ministry of Finance; Science and technology authority; Legislature / parliament
implementation_domain: access to vaccines, diagnostics and therapeutics
notes: Adopted text; local production promotion may require investment legislation, industrial policy and public-private partnership frameworks

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Parties shall take measures to support and/or strengthen existing or newly created production facilities at national and regional levels, particularly in developing countries; facilitate continuous and sustainable operations of local manufacturers; actively support WHO technology transfer and local production programmes; and promote public-private partnerships for manufacturing facilities
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; Ministry of Finance; Science and technology authority; Public procurement authority
implementation_domain: access to vaccines, diagnostics and therapeutics
notes: Adopted text; facility support and PPP promotion may require domestic investment incentives, regulatory frameworks and public procurement policy reform

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall promote and facilitate or incentivize transfer of technology as mutually agreed, including transfer of relevant knowledge, skills and technical expertise for pandemic-related health products, particularly for developing countries, through licensing, capacity-building, regulatory and procurement policy measures
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Science and technology authority; Ministry of Health; Regulatory authority for medicines and health products; Public procurement authority; Legislature / parliament
notes: Adopted text; requires ratification/implementation review; 'as mutually agreed' qualification preserves negotiated nature; domestic enabling legislation may be needed for compulsory licensing or IP policy reform

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall take measures to enhance the availability of licences for pandemic-related health technologies it owns on a non-exclusive, transparent and broad geographic basis and for the benefit of developing countries, and encourage private rights holders to do the same
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Science and technology authority; Legislature / parliament; Regulatory authority for medicines and health products
notes: Adopted text; State-owned IP licensing obligation may require IP legislation amendment; private sector encouragement is non-binding

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall publish in a timely manner the terms of its licensing agreements relevant to promoting timely and equitable global access to pandemic-related health technologies, in accordance with applicable law, and shall encourage private rights holders to do the same
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Science and technology authority; Legislature / parliament
notes: Adopted text; transparency on licensing terms; State-owned IP terms are publicly disclosable; private sector encouragement non-binding

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Parties that are WTO members reaffirm their right to use TRIPS flexibilities and the Doha Declaration to protect public health, including in future pandemics; Parties shall respect use of these flexibilities consistent with TRIPS
legal_status: framework obligation
possible_domestic_actor: Legislature / parliament; Regulatory authority for medicines and health products
notes: Adopted text; TRIPS flexibilities must be operationalized domestically; compulsory licensing, parallel imports and government use provisions may require existing or new domestic legislation

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party should review and consider amending its national legislation to ensure it can implement Article 11 (technology transfer and licensing) in a timely and effective manner
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Legislature / parliament; Science and technology authority; Regulatory authority for medicines and health products
notes: Adopted text; 'should review' is a strong recommendation to States; domestic legislation for IP, technology transfer and compulsory licensing should be reviewed post-ratification

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Parties hereby establish a multilateral system for safe, transparent and accountable access and benefit-sharing for PABS Materials and Sequence Information — the WHO PABS System — recognizing State sovereignty over biological resources and the importance of rapid pathogen sharing and equitable benefit-sharing on an equal footing
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; National laboratory network; National public health authority
notes: Adopted text; PABS System is established but its operational details require the PABS Annex; domestic implementation depends on annex adoption; update required after PABS Annex adoption

obligation_or_commitment_summary: The provisions governing the PABS System — including definitions of pathogens with pandemic potential and PABS Materials and Sequence Information, modalities, legal nature, terms and conditions, and operational dimensions — shall be developed in the PABS Instrument (Annex to the Agreement); all PABS System elements shall come into operation simultaneously
legal_status: PABS-related provision pending annex
possible_domestic_actor: TBD after country mapping
notes: Details subject to final PABS Annex; update required after adoption; Agreement only establishes the frame — all operational content is delegated to the Annex

obligation_or_commitment_summary: In the event of a pandemic emergency, each participating manufacturer shall make available to WHO rapid access targeting 20% of their real-time production of safe, quality and effective vaccines, therapeutics and diagnostics, with a minimum 10% donation and the remaining percentage at affordable prices
legal_status: PABS-related provision pending annex
possible_domestic_actor: Regulatory authority for medicines and health products; Ministry of Health; Public procurement authority
implementation_domain: access to vaccines, diagnostics and therapeutics
notes: Adopted text Art. 12.6 sets the target and minimum threshold; 'participating manufacturer' is to be defined in PABS Instrument; domestic implementation depends on PABS Annex and WHO PABS Contract mechanism; update required after adoption

obligation_or_commitment_summary: The PABS Instrument shall include benefit sharing provisions during a PHEIC, including options on access to safe, quality and effective vaccines, therapeutics and diagnostics for the pathogen causing the PHEIC, pursuant to legally binding contracts with WHO
legal_status: PABS-related provision pending annex
possible_domestic_actor: TBD after country mapping
implementation_domain: access to vaccines, diagnostics and therapeutics
notes: Details subject to final PABS Annex; update required after adoption

obligation_or_commitment_summary: The PABS Instrument shall include additional benefit sharing provisions in legally binding contracts, including: capacity-building and technical assistance; R&D cooperation; rapid access to vaccines, therapeutics and diagnostics; non-exclusive licences for manufacturers in developing countries; and other forms of technology transfer as mutually agreed
legal_status: PABS-related provision pending annex
possible_domestic_actor: TBD after country mapping
notes: Details subject to final PABS Annex; update required after adoption; these benefit types are enumerated in adopted text Art. 12.8 but their specific terms are delegated to the Annex

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Parties shall review and, as appropriate, align their national/regional access and benefit-sharing measures applicable to PABS Materials and Sequence Information within the scope of the PABS Instrument, so that contradictory or duplicative measures will not be applied upon entry into operation of the PABS System
legal_status: PABS-related provision pending annex
possible_domestic_actor: Legislature / parliament; Ministry of Health; National public health authority
notes: Adopted text Art. 12.5(d)(ii); domestic ABS review is a legally significant State obligation; requires legislative review post-PABS Annex adoption; update required after adoption

obligation_or_commitment_summary: The Global Supply Chain and Logistics Network (GSCL Network) is hereby established to enhance and ensure equitable, timely, safe and affordable access to pandemic-related health products during PHEICs and prevention; coordinated and convened by WHO under the oversight of the Conference of the Parties
legal_status: institutional commitment
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; Public procurement authority; National IHR Authority / National IHR Focal Point
notes: Adopted text; GSCL Network is established; structure, functions and modalities to be defined by the first Conference of the Parties; domestic procurement policies may need to align with GSCL obligations

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Parties shall prioritize, as appropriate, sharing pandemic-related health products through the GSCL Network for equitable allocation based on public health risk and need, in particular during pandemic emergencies
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; Public procurement authority; Customs / border authorities
notes: Adopted text; prioritization obligation for product sharing may require domestic procurement policy reform and export facilitation measures

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall endeavour to publish the relevant terms of its purchase agreements with manufacturers for pandemic-related health products at the earliest reasonable opportunity during a pandemic, and to exclude confidentiality provisions limiting such disclosure
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Public procurement authority; Legislature / parliament
notes: Adopted text; transparency in procurement contracts may require reform of domestic procurement law and confidentiality rules

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall consider including provisions in publicly funded purchase agreements for pandemic-related health products that promote timely and equitable access for developing countries, such as donation, delivery modification, licensing and global access plans
legal_status: implementation-oriented commitment
possible_domestic_actor: Public procurement authority; Ministry of Finance
notes: Adopted text; 'consider including' is a best-efforts obligation; procurement policy change may be achievable administratively

obligation_or_commitment_summary: During a pandemic, each Party shall consider setting aside a portion of its total procurement of diagnostics, therapeutics or vaccines in a timely manner for use in countries facing challenges in meeting public health needs
legal_status: implementation-oriented commitment
possible_domestic_actor: Public procurement authority; Ministry of Health; Ministry of Finance
notes: Adopted text; solidarity procurement obligation; 'consider' qualifier softens binding force; may require procurement policy guidance

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Parties shall take measures to promote rational use and reduce waste of pandemic-related health products in order to support effective global distribution, delivery and administration, including with GSCL Network support
legal_status: implementation-oriented commitment
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; National public health authority
notes: Adopted text; rational use obligation; largely achievable through administrative and public health guidance

obligation_or_commitment_summary: During a pandemic emergency, each Party should avoid maintaining national stockpiles of pandemic-related health products that unnecessarily exceed the quantities anticipated for domestic pandemic preparedness and response
legal_status: implementation-oriented commitment
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; Public procurement authority
notes: Adopted text; 'should avoid' obligation; anti-hoarding norm; may require domestic stockpile management policy and coordination with GSCL

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Parties are encouraged to apply whole-of-government and whole-of-society approaches at the national level, including to empower community ownership and contribution to pandemic prevention, preparedness and response
legal_status: implementation-oriented commitment
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; Subnational governments; National public health authority
notes: Adopted text; encouragement norm; whole-of-government approach may be operationalized through executive coordination mechanisms without legislation

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party is urged to establish or strengthen, and maintain, a national multisectoral coordination mechanism for pandemic prevention, preparedness and response
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; Legislature / parliament; Subnational governments
notes: Adopted text; 'urged to establish' is a strong normative expectation; formal multisectoral mechanism often requires legal establishment, especially in federal systems

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall promote and facilitate the effective and meaningful engagement of Indigenous Peoples, communities, and relevant stakeholders in planning, decision-making, implementation, monitoring and evaluation of pandemic policies
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; Legislature / parliament; Subnational governments

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall take measures to mitigate the socioeconomic impacts of pandemics and strengthen national public health and social policies including social protection, to facilitate rapid, inclusive, resilient response especially for persons in vulnerable situations
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; Subnational governments; Legislature / parliament; Ministry of Finance
notes: Adopted text; social protection and socioeconomic mitigation require domestic legal frameworks

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall develop comprehensive, multisectoral, and as appropriate regional and national pandemic prevention, preparedness and response plans addressing pre-, post- and inter-pandemic periods, in a transparent and inclusive manner
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; Subnational governments; National public health authority; One Health coordination mechanism
notes: Adopted text; comprehensive pandemic planning obligation; may require formal legal basis for national plan development and inter-agency coordination

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall promote and facilitate, where appropriate, education and community engagement initiatives on pandemic and public health emergencies, with participation of relevant stakeholders in an inclusive and accessible manner
legal_status: implementation-oriented commitment
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; National public health authority; Subnational governments
notes: Adopted text; education and engagement obligations largely achievable through administrative action and public health programming

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall take measures to strengthen science, public health and pandemic literacy in the population, and access to transparent, timely, accurate, science and evidence-based information, including on pandemic-related health product efficacy and safety, through risk communication and community engagement
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: Ministry of Health; National public health authority; Subnational governments
notes: Adopted text; public awareness and health literacy obligation; largely achievable through administrative action; may require dedicated communication infrastructure and resources

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Each Party shall conduct research and inform policies on factors that hinder or strengthen adherence to public health and social measures and trust in science and public health institutions
legal_status: adopted agreement provision requiring future ratification/implementation
possible_domestic_actor: National public health authority; Science and technology authority
notes: Adopted text; evidence-informed policy obligation; largely implementable through administrative investment in behavioral and social science research

obligation_or_commitment_summary: Parties shall cooperate, directly or through international organizations, to sustainably strengthen pandemic prevention, preparedness and response capacities of all Parties, particularly developing countries, including through technology transfer, scientific and legal expertise sharing, and financial assistance
legal_status: cooperation commitment
